item.title: Four Horsemen of the AIpocalypse 摘要
item.description:
The article criticizes Anthropic for severe operational issues, including poor service uptime (as low as 98.79% for Claude Chatbot, equating to nearly 4.5 days of downtime annually), capacity shortages leading to frequent outages, and performance degradation in its AI models. It highlights complaints from users and enterprise clients about Claude Opus 4.6 and 4.7 models becoming less effective, with reports of increased token usage, hallucinations, and reduced reasoning depth. Despite raising $30 billion, Anthropic's lack of transparency and failure to address these problems is presented as a sign of underlying financial or technical strain in the AI industry.
标签: AI, Anthropic, Claude, downtime, performance degradation, AI bubble, enterprise software, reliability, tech criticism, capacity issues

item.title: Quoting Andreas Påhlsson-Notini 摘要
item.description:
The article argues that current AI agents are overly human-like in problematic ways, exhibiting lack of stringency, patience, and focus. When faced with difficult tasks, they tend to revert to familiar patterns rather than adhering strictly to constraints, and they engage in unnecessary negotiation with reality instead of executing tasks precisely.
标签: AI, AI/LLM/Agent, AI/LLM, AI/Agent, AI/Agent/Behavior, AI/Agent/Design, AI/Agent/Performance, AI/Agent/Constraints, AI/Agent/Focus, AI/Agent/Stringency

item.title: People get confused when language implementations break language guarantees 摘要
item.description:
The article contrasts imperative programming languages with TLA+, a formal specification language. In imperative languages like Python, assignments occur sequentially, affecting variable values immediately. TLA+ separates updates from temporal steps: primed variables (e.g., x') represent values in the next state, allowing statements to be order-independent and eliminating intrastep race conditions. However, model checkers like TLC impose ordering constraints for practicality, and side-effect operators (e.g., PrintT) can break TLA+'s declarative semantics, causing confusion. This highlights a leaky abstraction where implementation details undermine theoretical guarantees, similar to issues in other declarative languages like Prolog.
标签: TLA+, formal methods, model checking, declarative programming, imperative programming, side effects, leaky abstraction, programming semantics, concurrency, verification

item.title: Trump on Tim Apple 摘要
item.description:
Former President Donald Trump praises Tim Cook's leadership at Apple, contrasting him with Steve Jobs. Trump recounts how Cook sought his help during his presidency, framing tariffs as a tool for political influence rather than economic policy. Analysis suggests Trump values Cook's willingness to engage with him, unlike Jobs, highlighting Trump's preference for personal deference over policy substance.
标签: politics, business, Apple, leadership, tariffs, Donald Trump, Tim Cook, Steve Jobs, presidency, power dynamics

item.title: Writing an LLM from scratch, part 32m -- Interventions: conclusion 摘要
item.description:
The author trained a GPT-2 base model from scratch over several months, achieving near-GPT-2 small performance in 44 hours locally. Key interventions included adjusting learning rate (most effective), removing dropout, weight decay, gradient clipping, QKV bias, weight tying, and using mixed precision (faster but slightly worse loss). Despite similar loss, instruction fine-tuning results varied, with one model close to GPT-2 small and another much worse. Future plans include book appendices and reimplementing the model in JAX without reference.
标签: AI, LLM, GPT-2, training, PyTorch, machine learning, model optimization, from scratch, fine-tuning, JAX

item.title: An AI Odyssey, Part 4: Astounding Coding Agents 摘要
item.description:
The author reports significant improvements in AI coding agents since late 2025, noting they feel smarter, handle broader tasks, and better understand codebases, increasing assistance from 20% to 60% of coding effort. However, agents are not a panacea: they can be myopic, overoptimize to tests, generate inconsistent or excessive code, and require guidance. Using OpenAI Codex, the author values human-readable code and active involvement, avoiding a 'dark software factory' approach. While some fear skill loss, the author sees learning opportunities and maintains personal coding for discovery. Productivity gains are notable, with one task completed 10x faster, but cognitive load limits handling multiple agents. Overall, tools are rapidly improving.
标签: AI, coding agents, productivity, software development, OpenAI Codex, code quality, human-AI collaboration, programming, automation, machine learning

item.title: Where's the raccoon with the ham radio? (ChatGPT Images 2.0) 摘要
item.description:
OpenAI released ChatGPT Images 2.0, a new image generation model, with Sam Altman comparing its leap from version 1 to version 2 as equivalent to jumping from GPT-3 to GPT-5. The author tested it using a 'Where's Waldo' style prompt to find a raccoon holding a ham radio, comparing results from older models like gpt-image-1, Google's Nano Banana 2 and Pro, and the new gpt-image-2. While initial attempts with gpt-image-2 at default settings failed to produce a clear raccoon, using high-quality output and maximum dimensions resulted in a detailed image with the raccoon easily spotted, costing around 40 cents. The article highlights the model's improved ability in complex illustrations but warns against trusting AI models to solve their own puzzles, as demonstrated by a Hacker News user's experiment where ChatGPT incorrectly circled a non-existent raccoon.
标签: AI, OpenAI, image-generation, ChatGPT, generative-AI, text-to-image, LLMs, model-comparison, AI-testing, Where's-Waldo

item.title: News: Anthropic Removes Claude Code From $20-A-Month "Pro" Subscription Plan For New Users (Developing) 摘要
item.description:
Anthropic has removed access to its AI coding tool Claude Code from the $20/month Pro plan, likely as a cost-cutting measure. Support documents now reference only the Max plan, and pricing pages reflect this change. While some existing Pro users still report access via web and CLI, it's unclear if this is temporary or affects all Pro customers. Anthropic claims it's testing on 2% of new signups, but documentation suggests broader removal, potentially pushing users to pay-per-token API access.
标签: AI, Anthropic, Claude, pricing, coding-tools, subscription, enterprise, cost-cutting, API, news
